title: phacelia lutea var. glandulifera
intro: Phacelia lutea var. glandulifera, also known as glandular phacelia, is a perennial plant species within the borage family (Boraginaceae) native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant qualities. The plant's health benefits include soothing coughs, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Herbal preparations of Phacelia lutea var. glandulifera can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to relieve cold and flu symptoms, as well as to support overall respiratory health.
